name: CareOne
tagline: An AI operating layer for healthcare administration.
summary: CareOne connects providers, payers, and reimbursement systems through a shared AI intelligence layer — automating eligibility, prior authorization, claim validation, denials, appeals, and payment across one explainable pipeline.
body: Healthcare administration is fragmented across disconnected EHRs, payer portals, clearinghouses, and manual work. CareOne addresses that fragmentation directly, operating as a single intelligence layer that spans the systems that were never designed to talk to one another.

The platform works through six coordinated stages — Verify, Authorize, Document, Validate, Protect, and Resolve — each passing explainable context to the next, so every decision can be traced and defended.

CareOne leverages Claude as its reasoning engine to read and interpret physician notes, payer policies, medical necessity criteria, contracts, and claim histories — powering specialized AI agents that do the work, rather than functioning as a chatbot.
The outcome is administrative throughput without the loss of accountability: faster eligibility and authorization, fewer denials, cleaner claims, and a documented rationale at every step.

name: ExecBoard
tagline: An AI operating layer for executive decision-making.
summary: ExecBoard gives leadership teams one defensible view of the decisions in front of them — connecting financials, operations, risk, and strategy through a shared intelligence layer that turns scattered reporting into board-ready judgment.
body: Every board cycle repeats the same scramble: reconciling decks, chasing owners, and re-deriving the same numbers under deadline. ExecBoard replaces that scramble with a single, continuously-maintained view of the organization's position.

The platform works through six coordinated stages — Gather, Reconcile, Frame, Model, Recommend, and Track — each passing explainable context to the next, so a recommendation always arrives with the reasoning and the alternatives beside it.

ExecBoard reads financial statements, operating metrics, contracts, and prior board minutes to brief executives with options and rationale rather than dashboards — powering specialized agents that prepare, pressure-test, and then follow each decision through to its outcome.
The outcome is a leadership team that spends its time deciding rather than assembling: less preparation, sharper options, and a clear record of why each call was made.

name: GeoShield
tagline: An AI operating layer for infrastructure resilience.
summary: GeoShield gives operators of critical infrastructure a live, geospatial picture of risk — connecting sensor networks, asset registries, weather, and field reporting through a shared intelligence layer that turns raw signals into coordinated response.
body: Critical infrastructure fails in the gaps between systems that were never designed to talk: SCADA, GIS, maintenance records, and emergency channels each hold part of the picture, and no one holds all of it. GeoShield assembles that picture in one place, continuously.

The platform works through six coordinated stages — Map, Monitor, Detect, Assess, Coordinate, and Restore — each passing explainable context to the next, so an alert always arrives with its location, its severity, and the action it calls for.

GeoShield interprets telemetry, inspection notes, and hazard models to anticipate failures and brief responders with prioritized, explainable actions — powering specialized agents rather than functioning as one more alerting dashboard.
The outcome is resilience that is operational rather than theoretical: failures seen earlier, responses coordinated faster, and a defensible record of every decision made under pressure.
